description: Putting a high-cardinality identifier in a tag/label, exploding series count and exhausting memory.
body: Failure mode: an identifier (user id, request id, full URL) is used as a tag/label, so every unique value creates a new series and the index grows until the instance OOMs or slows for everyone. Detection signals: series cardinality climbing without bound; memory pressure; a tag whose distinct-value count tracks traffic. Do not store identifiers in tags. Safer fallback: move the identifier to a field, enumerate allowed tag values, and re-measure cardinality.
